food_subgroup: Fish and rice porridge / congee with seafood

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03311069 covers congee generically. Goliath grouper fish porridge is a specific variant with grouper fish slices; closest_parent applied as no species-specific fish congee code exists in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers rice porridge/congee generically. No specific grouper fish congee code exists in FoodEx2; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for fish porridge or grouper congee. SNOMED CT does not enumerate individual prepared dish variants at this level of specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of goliath grouper fish porridge as served at a Singapore seafood soup restaurant, approximately 130g, containing rice porridge base with fish slices and broth.

nutrient_source.title: Fish Congee with Fish Slices — Singapore Food Composition Database, Health Promotion Board Singapore; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Grouper, mixed species, raw (NDB No. 15032)
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Singapore Food Composition Database provides nutrient data for fish congee (rice porridge with fish) as the closest approved Singaporean dish match. Goliath grouper (Epinephelus lanceolatus) macronutrient profile is closely analogous to other grouper species documented in HPB and USDA SR Legacy databases. Per-100g values are adapted from HPB fish congee data and cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy entry 15032 (grouper, mixed species, raw) to derive a composite nutrient profile. The 130g serving weight is the stated portion size from the input record and is used only for glycemic load calculation, not as a nutrient source.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Rice congee / rice porridge entry; corroborated by University of Sydney GI Database rice porridge listing.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for goliath grouper fish porridge. Rice congee/porridge has a published GI of approximately 57 (medium) in the University of Sydney GI Database and the International Tables of GI Values (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283). Fish slices and broth additions do not substantially alter the GI of the rice base. Glycemic load per 130g serving calculated as: GL = (GI x available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (57 x 11.7g) / 100 = 6.7. Available carbohydrate per serving = 9.0g per 100g x 130g = 11.7g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 310mg per 100g (403mg per 130g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that a 130g serving contributes approximately 403mg sodium (~17% of the 2,300mg daily limit); those on strict sodium restriction (<1,500mg/day) should limit portion size or request reduced-sodium preparation and avoid adding soy sauce or fish sauce at the table.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 57) from rice porridge base with moderate carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: The medium GI of rice porridge (GI ~57) and a glycemic load of approximately 6.7 per 130g serving make this a moderate-impact carbohydrate choice; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size, pair with additional protein or non-starchy vegetables to blunt postprandial glucose rise, and prefer this dish over higher-GI plain white rice dishes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat, lean fish protein, and omega-3 fatty acids from grouper
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Goliath grouper is a lean fish with low saturated fat (~0.3g/100g) and provides beneficial omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids; this dish is generally cardioprotective when consumed without excessive added sodium or oil, and lean fish consumption is consistent with heart-healthy dietary patterns recommended by cardiovascular guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Goliath Grouper Fish Porridge (龙胆鱼片泡饭) is a Singaporean-Chinese seafood congee dish featuring sliced goliath grouper (Epinephelus lanceolatus), one of the largest reef fish in the world, served in a slow-simmered rice porridge base. The dish is prepared by cooking white rice in fish stock or water until the grains break down into a thick, silky congee, then gently poaching fresh grouper slices in the hot porridge to preserve their delicate texture. The goliath grouper is prized for its thick, collagen-rich skin and firm yet tender white flesh, which imparts a rich umami depth to the broth. The porridge is typically seasoned with ginger, white pepper, sesame oil, and light soy sauce, and garnished with spring onions and fried shallots. This dish is prepared using a traditional recipe that emphasises the natural sweetness of the fish and the comforting warmth of the congee. Nutritionally, the dish provides approximately 68 kcal per 100g, with 6.2g protein and only 1.1g fat, making it a low-calorie, high-protein, lean seafood meal. The sodium content is moderate at approximately 310mg per 100g, which warrants attention for individuals managing hypertension. The medium glycemic index (~57) from the rice base means blood glucose impact is moderate, and the glycemic load per 130g serving is approximately 6.7, which is low. The dish is suitable for individuals seeking a light, nourishing, protein-rich meal and is consistent with heart-healthy dietary patterns due to its lean fish content and low saturated fat profile. It is commonly consumed as a breakfast, lunch, or dinner dish and is regarded as a comforting and restorative meal in Singaporean Chinese culinary tradition.
common_user_queries: Is fish porridge good for diabetics?
What does goliath grouper taste like?
Goliath grouper fish porridge nutrition facts

confidence_reason: No exact entry for goliath grouper fish porridge exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore Food Composition Database fish congee data and cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy grouper fish data (NDB No. 15032), both approved primary sources. The GI is an analogous estimate from published rice congee GI values in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). Confidence is medium because the dish composition is well-understood but the exact restaurant recipe and precise ingredient ratios are not available from an approved primary source.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted estimates based on HPB Singapore Food Composition Database fish congee data and USDA SR Legacy grouper fish profile (NDB No. 15032); no exact entry for goliath grouper porridge exists in any approved primary database. 2. The 130g serving weight is the stated portion size from the input record and is used only for glycemic load calculation. 3. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB fish congee source data; dish-level vitamin D value is not available from an approved source. 4. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB fish congee source data. 5. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB fish congee source data. 6. Trans fat is 0.0; confirmed negligible in lean fish and plain rice porridge with no added hydrogenated fats. 7. GI of 57 is an analogous estimate from rice congee published GI values (University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283); no direct GI measurement for goliath grouper fish porridge exists. 8. Goliath grouper (Epinephelus lanceolatus) is listed as Critically Endangered by the IUCN; its availability in Singapore may be subject to regulatory and sustainability considerations. 9. Halal status is assumed based on the fish-based nature of the dish and absence of pork or alcohol in the described recipe; formal halal certification of the restaurant has not been verified.
